old_claim.note: Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Maritime Pilot Books and Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; Written sailing directions and pilot books are a textual navigation aid and should not depend on the later visual chart form.

source_shape.source_locator: Research guide identifies portolans, rutters, sea-atlases, sailing directions, and pilot books as written aids for navigation, with the earliest written books of sailing directions focused on the Mediterranean.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Written sailing directions and pilot books are a textual navigation aid and should not depend on the later visual chart form.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Maritime Pilot Books claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Maritime Pilot Books treated Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Maritime Pilot Books no longer directly depends on Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: maritime_pilot_books must not directly depend on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows maritime_pilot_books is intentionally scoped only to cases that require portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
The graph splits maritime_pilot_books and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
A stronger source demonstrates that portolan_charts_maritime_navigation is chronologically and causally necessary for maritime_pilot_books.
short_reason: Written sailing directions and pilot books are a textual navigation aid and should not depend on the later visual chart form.
